title: [lora_l2.c:259] ssize_t to int cast on error path
description:
What: generate_eui64() stores hwinfo_get_device_id() return (ssize_t) in hwid_len, then casts to int for logging and return:
```c
ssize_t hwid_len;
hwid_len = hwinfo_get_device_id(hwid, sizeof(hwid));
if (hwid_len < 0) {
    LOG_ERR("...", (int)hwid_len);  // cast
    ret = (int)hwid_len;  // cast
}
```

Why it matters: On platforms where ssize_t is larger than int (e.g., 64-bit), large negative error codes could be truncated. However:
1. Zephyr hwinfo returns standard errno codes (small negative integers)
2. LICHEN targets 32-bit ARM where int == ssize_t size
3. Error codes fit in int on all platforms

This is a theoretical portability concern, not a practical bug.

Evidence: Line 264 cast (int)hwid_len
close_reason: Added comment explaining ssize_t to int cast is safe: hwinfo errors are negative errno values which always fit in int
